reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading verified: deposit validation errors are wrapped as execReqErr at transition_no_verify_sig.go:95, then detected at receive_block.go:516 and returned raw instead of wrapped in invalidBlock (L517). Spec cross-reference confirmed (osaka-requests SG-001 INV-005: 'Every validation failure terminates with InvalidBlock exception'). However, attack impact is narrower than claimed. The raw error still causes ReceiveBlock to abort (L110-112), so invalid blocks ARE rejected locally. The vulnerability is that without invalidBlock wrapping, fork choice pruning via handleInvalidExecutionError (process_block.go:1023) is not triggered, so the invalid block root isn't marked for pruning in fork choice. This could allow the same invalid block to be reprocessed or affect fork choice weight calculations, but does NOT allow invalid state transitions to finalize. Severity calibrated to Medium: Prysm has 31% network share (BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json), which is below the 33% threshold for High severity and above 5% for Medium. A single-client bug affecting deposit validation cannot impact >33% of the network.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: osaka-requests SG-001 INV-005: Every validation failure terminates with InvalidBlock exception
